nodes.title: Advanced Photography
nodes.description: Continuation of ARTS 2510 and ARTS 2520, exploring the implications of photography and encouraging students to use the medium to better understand their relationship to the world. Prerequisites: ARTS 2510 or ARTS 2520; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior standing; SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the week of registration; SART, ARTH, ARTS minors will be able to enroll, space permitting, the Friday afternoon of the week of registration; PACE students with permission and override.

nodes.title: Printing in Public Spaces
nodes.description: Exploration of various themes and/or media. Representative topics: Making Artist's Books; Gesamkunstwerk from Scratch. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS, ARTH minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Minimum sophomore standing. PACE students with permission and override. Fee: $100.00

nodes.title: Color Photography
nodes.description: Use of digital cameras, Adobe Photoshop, and inkjet printing processes as means for description, analysis, and expression of experience. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS, ARTH and RDS minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Minimum sophomore standing. PACE students with permission and override. Fee: $120.00

nodes.title: Clay: Wheel Throwing
nodes.description: Development of throwing skills and the capacity to create a range of forms. Investigation of surface treatment techniques such as slip painting and glazing. Low-fire and stoneware firing. Related clay and glaze technology. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS , ARTH minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Minimum sophomore standing. Fee: $55.00 PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Advanced Sculpture
nodes.description: Advanced investigation of sculpture. Students develop a personal and disciplined approach to making art through independent exploration within a structured environment. Students design individual projects that include aspects of research and writing. Group discussion and analysis of work are ongoing. Prerequisite: ARTS 2750, ARTS 2710, or ARTS 2720; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing. SART, AHST and ARTE majors only until after the third day of registration; then ARTS and AHST minors may also enroll; beginning Monday April 13th any student may enroll space permitting.

nodes.title: Professional Practices
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Clay: Hand Building
nodes.description: Investigation of surfaces and three-dimensional forms. Focus on variety of construction methods, surface treatment, and firing techniques. Related clay and glaze technology. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS , ARTH minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Minimum sophomore standing; PACE students with permission and override.

nodes.title: Painting: Color and Invention
nodes.description: Exploration of the role of color in painting. Projects will foster comprehension of color vocabulary and a critical understanding necessary for the effective use of color. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS, ARTH minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Minimum sophomore standing. PACE students with permission and override. Fee: $310.00

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory-level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Sophomore standing; Application required, seehttps://www.uvm.edu/cas/art/student-forms-and-contractsfor application form.

nodes.title: Advanced Drawing
nodes.description: Intense investigations of drawing and elements that relate to that discipline. Emphasis on conceptual method, contemporary techniques, and both objective and non-objective source material. Prerequisite: ARTS 2100, ARTS 2110, or ARTS 2220; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing; SART, AHST ARTE majors only during the week of registration; SART, ARTH, ARTS minors will be able to enroll, space permitting, the Friday afternoon of the week of registration; PACE students with permission and override. Minimum Junior standing; Fee: $105.00

nodes.title: Projects in Painting
nodes.description: Further exploration of formal and conceptual concerns through studio work and critique. Each student will develop a coherent body of paintings. Prerequisite: ARTS 2100, ARTS 2210, ARTS 2200, or ARTS 2220; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior standing; SART, AHST and ARTE majors only until after the third day of registration; then ARTS and AHST minors may also enroll; beginning Monday April 13th any student may enroll space permitting. PACE students by permission and override. Fee $310

nodes.title: SL:INTN: Comm Engaged Arts
nodes.description: Service-learning course in which students will complete a semester-long internship at a community field site along with a weekly seminar that supports community engaged work. Internship sites are individualized based on students' interests and may include galleries, art studios, arts advocacy programs, afterschool programs, or other related sites. Prerequisites: ARTS 1100 or ARTS 1400; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing. Instructor permission required, Art Education students only, except by permission, space permitting

nodes.title: Drawing
nodes.description: Introductory study of visual experience through drawing and its transformation of the three-dimensional visual world onto a two-dimensional surface. Emphasis varies with Instructor. Credit not awarded for both ARTS 1100 and ARTS 1011.

nodes.title: Internship
nodes.description: On-site supervised work experience combined with a structured academic learning plan directed by a faculty member or a faculty-staff team in which a faculty member is the instructor of record, for which academic credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion. Prerequisites: Six hours of Studio Art courses at the 2000-level; Junior standing; departmental permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Junior standing; departmental permission. Prereq: Six hours 2000-level ARTS; Faculty sponsor; Minimum Junior standing; Application required, seehttps://www.uvm.edu/cas/art/student-forms-and-contractsfor application form.

nodes.title: Digital Art and Ecology
nodes.description: Topics exploring themes in digital art at the intermediate level. Representative topics: Art & Intelligent Machines. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the week of registration; CAS ARTS, ARTH minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Minimum sophomore standing. PACE students with permission and override. Fee: $100.00

nodes.title: Graphic Design
nodes.description: The application of graphic design principles to practical problems, including the impact of popular design on society, and the exploration of visual elements in contemporary printing processes. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, ARTH, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS, ARTH minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Minimum sophomore standing. PACE students with permission and override. Fee: $45.00

nodes.title: Contemporary Cinema
nodes.description: Introduction to basic film history, theory, and analytical skills. An historical overview of contemporary international cinema.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Faculty sponsor; Minimum Junior standing; Application required, seehttps://www.uvm.edu/cas/art/student-forms-and-contractsfor application form.

nodes.title: Printmaking: Silkscreen
nodes.description: Studio class focusing on procedures in stencil printing that use photo-silkscreen technology. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400.

nodes.title: Sound Art
nodes.description: Students will work with interdisciplinary approaches to art subjects. Representative topics: Multi-Media Documentary; Socially-Engaged Art. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS, ARTH minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Minimum sophomore standing. PACE students with permission and override. Fee: $140.00

nodes.title: Animation for Artists
nodes.description: Topics exploring themes in film and video at the intermediate level. Students will work with non-traditional approaches to moving image arts and the dialogues around them. Representative topic: Animation for Artists. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, ARTS 1400, ARTS 1600, FTS 1020, or FTS 1430.
nodes.other_requirements:
SART, AHST and ARTE majors only until after the third day of
registration; then ARTS and AHST minors may also enroll; 
beginning Monday April 13th any student may enroll space
permitting.
Minimum sophomore standing;
PACE students by permission and override.
Fee: $73.00

nodes.title: Figure Drawing
nodes.description: Topics exploring themes in drawing at the intermediate level. Students will learn and experience non-traditional approaches to drawing and the dialogues around them. Representative topic: Experimental Drawing. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST and ARTE majors only until after the third day of registration; then ARTS and AHST minors may also enroll; beginning Monday April 13th any student may enroll space permitting.

nodes.title: Projects in Printmaking
nodes.description: Students conceive, research, develop, and realize their own projects in the print studio. Prerequisites: ARTS 2300, ARTS 2310, or ARTS 2320; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Minimum Junior standing; SART, AHST,ARTE majors only during the week of registration SART, ARTH, ARTS minors will be able to enroll, space permitting, the Friday afternoon of the week of registration PACE students with permission and override. Fee: $150.00

nodes.title: Photography
nodes.description: Introduction to making black-and-white photographs, emphasizing craft and conceptual problem solving. Students gain skill in camera operation, printing, and producing work of an individual nature. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, ARTS 1400, FTS 1420, or FTS 1430.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS, ARTH and RDS minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Minimum sophomore standing. PACE students with permission and override. Fee: $120.00

nodes.title: Internship: Student Teaching
nodes.description: Provides an opportunity for continuous contacts with the same group of students over a relatively long period of time. In addition, candidates continually interact with field site personnel in various phases of the total program. Provides a directed and supervised field experience. Prerequisites: Senior standing in Art Education major, PRAXIS Core, completion of all course requirements, GPA of at least 3. Co-requisites: EDSC 5230.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior standing in Art Education major, PRAXIS Core, completion of all course requirements, GPA of at least 3. Instructor permission.

nodes.title: Teaching Internship Seminar
nodes.description: Supports ARTS 4881 Internship: Student Teaching. Addresses pertinent issues in preK-12 art education teaching and learning, while preparing students to construct their licensure portfolios and enter the profession in school and community settings. Prerequisites: ARTS 2881 Sections A and B, ARTS 4870. Co-requisite: ARTS 4881.
nodes.other_requirements: ARTE majors only; Senior standing; Enroll in conjunction with ARTS 4881 Section A

nodes.title: Painting: Observation & Image
nodes.description: Exploration of the formal and conceptual practices of painting. Introduction of historical genres and issues in painting such as still life, figuration, and abstraction. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS, ARTH minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Minimum sophomore standing. PACE students with permission and override. Fee: $310.00

nodes.title: Cur&Practice: Elementary Art
nodes.description: Study and implementation of curriculum. Students work directly in a school classroom. Lectures and discussions. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: Eighteen hours in Studio Art; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: Eighteen hours in Studio Art; minimum Junior standing. Art Education majors only; Junior standing; Course fee: $25

nodes.title: Honors
nodes.description: College honors thesis or other department/program honors, under the supervision of a faculty member. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission, For CAS students pursuing College Honors

nodes.title: Digital Fabrication
nodes.description: Hands-on experience with digital fabrication technologies (both hardware and software) that are popular with contemporary artists; opportunities to practice design iteration and rapid prototyping and experiment with art-making practices such as tessellation, 3D Imaging, generative design, artificial life and interaction design. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400; or Computer Science major or College of Engineering & Mathematical Sciences student; minimum Junior standing. Cross-listed with: CIS 2460.
nodes.other_requirements: Computer Science major or College of Engineering & Mathematical Sciences student; minimum Junior standing. SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS, ARTH minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Fee: $55; PACE students with permission and override.

nodes.title: Intermediate Drawing
nodes.description: Intensive investigation of drawing and elements related to the discipline. Focus on expanding techniques and developing strategies for making drawings. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS , ARTH minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Minimum sophomore standing. PACE students with permission and override. Fee: $105.00

nodes.title: Current Issues in Art Ed
nodes.description: Research and discussion of issues relevant to contemporary art and the teaching of art. May be repeated for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: Senior standing or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Senior standing or Instructor permission; Art Education Majors only. Course fee: $25.00

nodes.title: Classical Cinema
nodes.description: Introduction to basic film history, theory, and analytical skills. An historical overview of classical international cinema.

nodes.title: Top in Printmaking Risograph
nodes.description: Topics exploring themes in printmaking at the intermediate level. Students will work with approaches to the print and the dialogues around them, beyond what is covered in our customary offerings. Representative topics: Printmaking: Risograph; Printmaking: Woodcut. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS , ARTH minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Minimum sophomore standing; PACE students with permission and override. Fee: $120.00

nodes.title: Advanced Digital Art
nodes.description: Advanced exploration of the computer as an artistic medium for creating imagery. Focus on using the computer to animate images and integrate sound. Emphasis on conceptual issues in digital art. Prerequisites: ARTS 2400, ARTS 2410, ARTS 2520, ARTS 2600, or ARTS 2610; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: minimum Junior standing. SART, AHST and ARTE majors only until after the third day of registration; then ARTS and AHST minors may also enroll; beginning Monday April 13th any student may enroll space permitting. Some experience with basic concepts in digital art is expected.

nodes.title: Perspectives on Art Making
nodes.description: Introduction to contemporary art practice in various media. Explores method and meaning in art making, the role of experimentation, and the translation of experience into artwork. Credit not awarded for both ARTS 1400 and ARTS 1014.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS, ARTH minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed.

nodes.title: Representing Landscapes
nodes.description: Topics exploring themes in painting at the intermediate level. Students will learn and work through technical, formal, and conceptual issues about painting. Representative topics: Representing Landscapes; Collage and Assemblage; Watercolor Painting. May repeat for credit with different content. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years. Prerequisite: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS SART, ARTH minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Minimum sophomore standing. PACE students with permission and override. Course fee: $225.00

nodes.title: Sculpture
nodes.description: Introduction to making and critiquing sculpture. Using visual elements of sculpture and concepts of 3D design, students establish a foundation for individualized inquiry and experimentation. Conceptual, practical, and analytical skills are developed through presentations, research, writing, problem solving, and critiques. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, or ARTS 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST, ARTE majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS, ARTH minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Minimum sophomore standing. PACE students with permission and override. Fee: $130.00

nodes.title: FYS Perspectives on Art Making
nodes.description: Introduction to contemporary art practice in various media. Explores method and meaning in art making, the role of experimentation, and the translation of experience into artwork. Emphasis on developing critical reading and writing skills, substantive revision, information literacy, and analytical thinking.

nodes.title: Introduction to Video Art
nodes.description: Study of the conceptual and technical aspects of experimental and avant-garde film and video through exercises, viewing, reading and discussion, and creating films. Prerequisites: ARTS 1010, ARTS 1011, ARTS 1014, ARTS 1100, ARTS 1400, ARTS 1600, FTS 1420, or FTS 1430.
nodes.other_requirements: SART, AHST, ARTE, FTS majors only during the first week of registration; CAS ARTS, ARTH and RDS, FTS minors only for the following 3 days; Non-CAS minors for the following 2 days; then all restrictions will be removed; Minimum sophomore standing. PACE students with permission and override. Fee: $45.00
